text:
There is a subtle excitement in walking through these bright, open spaces. The first room, behind glass, is quiet—an invitation to contemplation and potential discussion. The air is full of possibility, sterile yet brimming with the anticipation of collective thought.

In the second scene, shapes and colors on the wall pulse with the promise of innovation, evoking the energy of inquiry. The idea of research and development—R+D—carries both the thrill of the unknown and a strange comfort, as though I am part of a larger movement toward progress. I feel a deep curiosity here, as if standing at the threshold of discovery, quietly aware of both my insignificance and my undeniable involvement in something transformative.
